title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses Meta's vision for AI-powered smart glasses as the next computing platform. The conversation covers AI assistants for daily tasks, privacy concerns around facial recognition, on-device AI, and how smart glasses could reduce smartphone dependency through real-world contextual awareness.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz interviews Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, about the case for AI-powered smart glasses becoming the computing platform that succeeds the smartphone. The discussion lays out candidate use cases (task assistance, meetings, and real-world context awareness) and maps the competitive field against OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon. It also addresses privacy questions raised by on-device facial recognition and AI, which matter as wearables move sensing and conversational AI onto the face.
